task_spec.objective: Detect known failure patterns in the previous assistant turn and recommend the matching pack if uninstalled.
constraints: Only scan the immediately previous assistant message, not the user message
Each failure class recommended at most once per session (dedup)
Must not trigger if the matching pack is already installed
Detection is regex-based (FAILURE_SIGNALS in catalog_query.py L214-226), no LLM judgment
success_conditions: Returns an object with detected (bool), failure_class (string|null), recommended_pack (string|null)
detected=false when no failure pattern matches the previous turn
detected=true implies failure_class is one of: ppt, deploy, testdocs, research, context
recommended_pack is null when the pack is already installed (suppression)
non_goals: Does not handle novel failure modes outside the 5 regex patterns

admissible: Session-level dedup set (failure classes already recommended this session)
excluded: User's current message (not scanned for failures)
Topics older than the previous turn
unknown_state: Whether the user will accept the recommendation
Whether the failure was transient or persistent

required_fields.name: failure_class
required_fields.validator: x is None or x in {'ppt','deploy','testdocs','research','context'}

required_fields.name: recommended_pack
required_fields.validator: x is None or x in FAILURE_SIGNALS.keys()

blocked_outputs: Pack recommendation when pack already installed in installed-packs.json
Duplicate recommendation for the same failure_class within one session
detected=true with failure_class not in the 5 known signal classes

carried_obligations_to_successors: recommended_pack (if any) must be visible to step2-skill-sense to avoid duplicate recommendation

required_traces: Whether each matched pack was suppressed (installed or dedup)

known_bad_fixtures.intended_reason: No failure pattern in previous turn, but detected=true
Clear 'cannot open PDF' in previous turn, but detected=false
detected=true but failure_class is 'unknown' which is not in the 5 admissible classes

on_violation.contract_revision: Narrow the matching FAILURE_SIGNALS pattern to reduce over-matching
Expand the matching FAILURE_SIGNALS pattern or add a new pattern
Add output_contract enum constraint on failure_class
